reason: RAG Orchestrator server modules, scripts, and environment contracts must follow existing backend ownership boundaries.
Orchestrator and assistant adapter failures must return safe JSON errors and fall back without leaking internals.
RAG diagnostics and provider failures must be low-sensitive and must not log keys, endpoints, prompts, or private data.
RAG health, metrics, and status integration must use safe labels and default-closed production gates.
Assistant response shape, citations, and metadata must remain typed and compatible across UI/API/fallback paths.
Phase 2 must build on the confirmed Agentic Hybrid RAG architecture and preserve the local fallback boundary.
